title: Testing, Release Gates, and Memory Incident Defense · C Contract Brief: Compiler Target Matrix
summary: A critical native agent must ship a memory rewrite with compatibility, rollback, observability, and practiced incident recovery. Planned artifact: Add and verify the cmem compiler target matrix increment in production memory-safety defense dossier. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Test supported GCC and Clang versions, optimization modes, architectures, libraries, feature macros, and warning baselines.
Combine unit, property, integration, fault, stress, sanitizer, fuzz, ABI, resource, load, and restore evidence by risk.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Compiler Target Matrix · Theory Compiler Target Matrix
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory native-systems team handles memory case 9155. Produce bounded C evidence to decide release readiness from complementary memory evidence; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Test supported GCC and Clang versions, optimization modes, architectures, libraries, feature macros, and warning baselines.

steps.title: Build Compiler Target Matrix evidence · Theory Compiler Target Matrix
steps.instruction:
For this theory demonstration of cmem-compiler-target-matrix, write the complete compile-ready C program headed "/* Evidence: cmem-compiler-target-matrix-1hmsx9 */" for changed case 2499. It must decide release readiness from complementary memory evidence, reject one changed boundary, print an observable result and invariant, and return nonzero when the invariant fails. Keep instant code within the PicoC 3.2.2 practice subset; do not claim C23, optimizing compiler, sanitizer, ABI, thread, operating-system, FFI, load, or production evidence until its explicit controlled transfer gate passes.
